chat.title: AI Symptom Check
chat.disclaimer: This is not a medical diagnosis. When in doubt, see a doctor.

children.title: My children
children.name: Name
children.empty: No children yet. Add your first child to start.

script.greeting: Hi! I'm your PediCare assistant. What's bothering your child today?
script.askDuration: Got it. How long has it been going on?
script.askSeverity: Is your child unusually drowsy, refusing fluids, or having trouble breathing?
script.verdictGreen: Based on your answers this looks manageable at home: rest, fluids and monitoring. If anything changes, run the check again.
script.verdictYellow: I recommend showing your child to a pediatrician within 24 hours. I can connect you now.
script.verdictRed: These signs need urgent attention. Please connect to a pediatrician right now or call emergency services.

landing.tourTitle: Built for both sides of care
landing.tourSubtitle: One platform. Two experiences, each designed for its user.
landing.q1: At 2 AM the AI told me exactly when to worry and when not to. We saw a doctor 20 minutes later.
landing.q2: The triage queue means I open my laptop and the sickest child is already on top. Scribe writes my notes.
landing.q3: We replaced four tools with PediCare. Billing codes itself now.

landing.p2D: Talk to the AI like to a nurse — it listens and answers
landing.p3D: Describe what happened — get what it might be and what to do

voice.subtitle: Talk to the AI assistant — it listens and answers like a triage nurse
voice.hint: Tap the microphone and say what's bothering your child. The assistant transcribes you live and answers right away.
voice.p1: My daughter has had a fever since last night, around 38.5, and she keeps coughing.
voice.a1: Got it. Is she drinking fluids well, and is she her usual self or unusually sleepy?
voice.p2: She drinks okay, but she's more tired than usual and naps a lot.
voice.a2: Fever with cough for under a day usually means a viral infection. Keep her hydrated, give weight-dosed paracetamol above 38.5°C and let her rest. Since the tiredness is unusual, I'd show her to a pediatrician within 24 hours.
voice.sent: Case sent to the triage queue

advisor.subtitle: Describe in your own words what happened — the AI suggests what it might be and what to do
advisor.placeholder: Example: my son woke up with 38.9 fever, he's coughing and refuses breakfast...
advisor.e1: High fever since morning and a rash on the tummy
advisor.e2: Coughs at night for three days, no fever
advisor.e3: Vomited twice and is very sleepy
advisor.noMatch: I couldn't recognize specific symptoms. Add a few details — or try the guided chat instead.
advisor.lFever: Most often a viral infection — fever itself helps fight it.
advisor.aFever1: Offer fluids often: water, compote, breast milk.
advisor.wFever: Fever lasts over 3 days or goes above 40°C
advisor.lCough: Usually viral or post-nasal drip; night cough can come from a dry room.
advisor.aCough1: Humidify the bedroom and give warm drinks.
advisor.wCough: Wheezing, fast breathing, or cough beyond 2 weeks
advisor.lRash: Often viral or an allergic reaction to food or contact.
advisor.aRash1: Note what was eaten or touched in the last day.
advisor.aRash2: Don't scratch; light cotton clothing helps.
advisor.wRash: Rash with fever, swelling of lips, or spots that don't fade under a glass
advisor.lVomit: Usually a stomach virus; the danger is losing fluids.
advisor.aVomit1: Small sips of oral rehydration solution every few minutes.
advisor.aVomit2: Skip solid food until vomiting settles.
advisor.wVomit: Can't keep any fluids down, blood in vomit, or signs of dehydration
advisor.aDiarrhea1: Rehydration solution after each loose stool.
advisor.wDiarrhea: Blood in stool, no urine for 6+ hours, or lasts over 3 days
advisor.lEar: Possible middle-ear inflammation, common after colds.
advisor.aEar2: Keep the head slightly elevated when lying.
advisor.wEar: Pain lasts over 24 hours, discharge from the ear, or fever joins
advisor.lBreathing: Hard or fast breathing always needs a doctor's assessment.
advisor.aBreathing1: Sit the child upright and keep them calm.
advisor.aBreathing2: Open a window for fresh, cool air.
advisor.wBreathing: This is urgent — connect to a pediatrician right now
advisor.lDrowsy: Marked sleepiness with illness can signal dehydration or serious infection.
advisor.aDrowsy1: Try to wake and offer fluids immediately.
advisor.wDrowsy: This is urgent — connect to a pediatrician right now
advisor.aGen: Observe your child and re-check in a few hours.
advisor.wGen: Your parental instinct says something is wrong
